name: Common Cold
overview: A mild viral infection that affects the nose and throat, causing congestion, sore throat, and sneezing.

name: Flu
overview: A contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses that can lead to fever, body aches, and fatigue.
prevention: Avoid close contact with sick people
whenToSeeDoctor: High fever that does not respond to medication

name: Fever
overview: An elevated body temperature often signifying the immune system is fighting an infection or inflammation.
prevention: Stay up to date on vaccinations

name: Dengue
overview: A mosquito-borne viral infection that causes high fever, severe joint pain, and rash.
causes: Dengue virus carried by Aedes mosquitoes

name: Malaria
overview: A serious infectious disease spread by mosquito bites, causing cyclical fever, chills, and fatigue.
causes: Plasmodium parasites transmitted through mosquito bites

name: Typhoid
overview: A bacterial infection spread through contaminated food or water, causing prolonged fever and weakness.
riskFactors: Travel to areas with poor sanitation
whenToSeeDoctor: High fever lasting more than 3 days

name: Food Poisoning
overview: A digestive illness caused by eating contaminated food or drink, often leading to nausea and diarrhea.
causes: Bacteria such as E. coli, Salmonella, or Campylobacter

name: Diarrhea
overview: Frequent loose or watery stools that may result from infection, food sensitivity, or digestive conditions.

name: Constipation
overview: Infrequent or difficult bowel movements that can be eased by hydration, fiber, and movement.
whenToSeeDoctor: Constipation lasting more than three weeks

name: Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)
overview: A bacterial infection in the urinary system that can cause pain, urgency, and discomfort during urination.
homeCare: Use a heating pad for pelvic pain
whenToSeeDoctor: Pain in the back or side

name: Stomach Ulcer
overview: An open sore in the lining of the stomach or upper small intestine that can cause burning pain and indigestion.

name: Migraine
overview: A neurological condition marked by intense, recurring headaches often accompanied by nausea or light sensitivity.

name: Sinusitis
overview: Inflammation of the sinuses often caused by infection, allergies, or nasal irritation.

name: Asthma
overview: A chronic lung condition that causes airway narrowing, wheezing, coughing, and breathing difficulty.

name: Allergy
overview: An immune response to normally harmless substances like pollen, dust, or food, causing irritation and swelling.

name: Anemia
overview: A condition where the body has too few healthy red blood cells, causing fatigue and weakness.

name: Diabetes
overview: A metabolic condition in which the body struggles to regulate blood sugar levels effectively.

name: Hypertension (High Blood Pressure)
overview: A long-term condition where blood pressure is persistently elevated, increasing the risk of heart disease.

name: Bronchitis
overview: Inflammation of the bronchial tubes that causes coughing, mucus production, and breathing discomfort.

name: Pneumonia
overview: An infection that inflames air sacs in the lungs, producing cough, fever, and breathing difficulty.

name: COVID-19
overview: An infectious disease caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus, affecting the respiratory system and sometimes other organs.

name: Tonsillitis
overview: Swelling and irritation of the tonsils caused by viral or bacterial infection, often with sore throat.
prevention: Avoid close contact with sick people

name: Laryngitis
overview: Inflammation of the voice box that causes hoarseness and temporary loss of voice.
whenToSeeDoctor: Voice loss lasting more than two weeks

name: Pertussis (Whooping Cough)
overview: A highly contagious respiratory infection that causes violent coughing fits and difficulty breathing.
prevention: Avoid close contact with sick individuals

name: Tuberculosis
overview: A bacterial infection that mainly affects the lungs and can lead to chronic cough, fever, and weight loss.

name: Chickenpox
overview: A viral illness causing itchy blisters, fever, and fatigue, usually affecting children.

name: Measles
overview: A viral infection that causes fever, cough, conjunctivitis, and a distinctive spotted rash.

name: Mumps
overview: A viral disease that causes painful swelling of the salivary glands and fever.

name: Hepatitis A
overview: A liver infection caused by the hepatitis A virus, typically spread through contaminated food or water.

name: Hepatitis B
overview: A viral liver infection that may become chronic and lead to liver damage or cancer.
causes: Contact with infected blood or body fluids

name: Hepatitis C
overview: A viral infection that can cause chronic liver inflammation, scarring, and long-term liver damage.

name: Gastroenteritis
overview: An inflammation of the stomach and intestines that causes vomiting and diarrhea.

name: Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS)
overview: A chronic digestive disorder causing abdominal pain, bloating, and changes in bowel habits.

name: Acid Reflux
overview: A condition where stomach acid flows back into the esophagus, causing heartburn and irritation.

name: Gallstones
overview: Solid particles that form in the gallbladder and may cause pain, especially after eating fatty foods.

name: Pancreatitis
overview: Inflammation of the pancreas that causes upper abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting.

name: Celiac Disease
overview: An autoimmune disorder triggered by gluten intake, causing damage to the small intestine.

name: Appendicitis
overview: Inflammation of the appendix that causes sudden severe abdominal pain and requires urgent medical attention.
prevention: Seek care for unexplained abdominal pain
homeCare: Do not eat or drink if appendicitis is suspected

name: Hemorrhoids
overview: Swollen veins in the lower rectum or anus, often causing pain and bleeding during bowel movements.

name: Dehydration
overview: A loss of too much body fluid that can impair organ function and cause dizziness and weakness.

name: Kidney Stone
overview: A hard mineral deposit that can form in the kidneys and cause severe flank pain and urinary symptoms.

name: Urinary Incontinence
overview: Loss of bladder control leading to accidental urine leakage and frequent bathroom needs.

name: Osteoporosis
overview: A condition that weakens bones and increases fracture risk, especially in older adults.

name: Osteoarthritis
overview: A degenerative joint disease that causes pain, stiffness, and reduced range of motion.

name: Rheumatoid Arthritis
overview: A chronic autoimmune disease that causes joint inflammation, pain, and stiffness.

name: Gout
overview: A type of arthritis caused by uric acid crystal deposits in joints, often affecting the big toe.

name: Fibromyalgia
overview: A condition characterized by widespread musculoskeletal pain, fatigue, and sleep disturbances.

name: Back Pain
overview: Pain in the lower, middle, or upper back that can result from muscle strain, injury, or underlying conditions.

name: Tension Headache
overview: A common headache type causing constant pressure or tightness around the head.

name: Epilepsy
overview: A disorder marked by recurrent seizures due to abnormal brain activity.

name: Stroke
overview: A medical emergency caused by interrupted blood flow to the brain, resulting in sudden weakness or speech problems.

name: Parkinson Disease
overview: A progressive nervous system disorder that affects movement, causing tremors and stiffness.

name: Alzheimer Disease
overview: A progressive brain disorder that slowly destroys memory and thinking skills.

name: Multiple Sclerosis
overview: An autoimmune disease that disrupts communication between the brain and body, causing neurological symptoms.

name: Anxiety Disorder
overview: A mental health condition characterized by persistent worry, nervousness, and physical tension.

name: Depression
overview: A mood disorder that causes persistent sadness, loss of interest, and fatigue.

name: Bipolar Disorder
overview: A mental health condition with alternating periods of mania and depression.

name: Insomnia
overview: A sleep disorder that makes it hard to fall asleep, stay asleep, or get restful rest.

name: Panic Disorder
overview: A condition marked by recurrent panic attacks, sudden intense fear, and physical symptoms.

name: Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD)
overview: A mental health condition triggered by a terrifying event, causing flashbacks and severe anxiety.

name: Schizophrenia
overview: A chronic mental disorder affecting perception, thoughts, and emotional expression.

name: Eczema
overview: A chronic skin condition causing itchy, inflamed patches on the skin.

name: Psoriasis
overview: An autoimmune skin disease that causes red, scaly patches and inflammation.

name: Acne
overview: A common skin condition that causes pimples, blackheads, and inflamed spots.

name: Dermatitis
overview: A broad term for skin irritation that causes redness, itching, and rash.

name: Rosacea
overview: A chronic skin condition that causes facial redness, visible blood vessels, and bumps.

name: Skin Infection
overview: An infection of the skin caused by bacteria, viruses, fungi, or parasites.

name: Melanoma
overview: A dangerous form of skin cancer that develops from pigment-producing cells.

name: Basal Cell Carcinoma
overview: A common skin cancer that usually grows slowly and appears on sun-exposed areas.
homeCare: See a doctor for suspicious lesions

name: Breast Cancer
overview: A malignant tumor that forms in breast tissue and may spread to other parts of the body.

name: Prostate Cancer
overview: A cancer that forms in the prostate gland, often growing slowly and affecting urinary function.

name: Colon Cancer
overview: A malignancy in the large intestine that may cause changes in bowel habits and blood in stool.

name: Leukemia
overview: A cancer of the blood or bone marrow that causes abnormal white blood cell production.

name: Lymphoma
overview: A cancer of the lymphatic system that may cause swollen lymph nodes, fever, and weight loss.

name: Chronic Kidney Disease
overview: Gradual loss of kidney function that can lead to fluid buildup and waste retention.

name: Acute Kidney Injury
overview: A sudden loss of kidney function that requires prompt medical attention.

name: Breastfeeding Issues
overview: Common challenges such as painful nipples, low milk supply, and blocked ducts during breastfeeding.

name: Menstrual Cramps
overview: Painful uterine contractions that occur during menstrual periods.

name: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)
overview: A hormonal disorder that can cause irregular periods, weight changes, and ovarian cysts.

name: Endometriosis
overview: A condition where tissue similar to the uterine lining grows outside the uterus, causing pain and fertility issues.
causes: Unknown, likely hormonal and immune factors

name: Menopause
overview: The natural end of menstruation, often accompanied by hot flashes, mood changes, and sleep issues.
homeCare: Talk to your doctor about hormone therapy

name: Male Pattern Baldness
overview: A common form of hair loss in men caused by genetic and hormonal factors.

name: Erectile Dysfunction
overview: The inability to achieve or maintain an erection sufficient for satisfactory sexual performance.

name: Dehydration
overview: A common condition resulting from losing more fluid than the body takes in.

name: Cold Sores
overview: Small, painful blisters around the mouth caused by the herpes simplex virus.

name: Ear Infection
overview: An infection of the middle ear that commonly affects children and causes ear pain.

name: Eye Strain
overview: Discomfort or fatigue caused by prolonged use of the eyes, especially when focusing.

name: Glaucoma
overview: A group of eye conditions that damage the optic nerve, often due to high eye pressure.

name: Pink Eye (Conjunctivitis)
overview: Inflammation of the eye lining that makes the eye red, itchy, and watery.

name: Toothache
overview: Pain in or around a tooth that may be caused by decay, infection, or dental injury.

name: Gingivitis
overview: A mild gum disease that causes inflammation, redness, and bleeding with brushing.

name: Dental Cavity
overview: Decay in a tooth caused by bacteria and acid that can lead to holes, pain, and infection.

name: Cold
overview: A common viral infection that affects the upper respiratory tract, causing runny nose and congestion.

name: Bacterial Vaginosis
overview: An imbalance of bacteria in the vagina that causes discharge, odor, and irritation.

name: Premenstrual Syndrome (PMS)
overview: A collection of physical and emotional symptoms that occur before a menstrual period.

name: Hypothyroidism
overview: A condition in which the thyroid gland does not produce enough hormones, slowing metabolism.

name: Hyperthyroidism
overview: An overactive thyroid causes an accelerated metabolism, leading to weight loss and anxiety.
prevention: Regular thyroid checks if at risk

name: Type 1 Diabetes
overview: An autoimmune condition in which the pancreas produces little or no insulin.

name: Type 2 Diabetes
overview: A metabolic disorder in which the body cannot use insulin properly, causing high blood sugar.

name: Gestational Diabetes
overview: High blood sugar diagnosed during pregnancy with potential risks for mother and baby.

name: Seasonal Allergic Rhinitis
overview: Also known as hay fever, it causes sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes during allergy seasons.

name: Sleep Apnea
overview: A sleep disorder characterized by repeated pauses in breathing during sleep.
homeCare: Use a CPAP machine if prescribed

name: Chronic Fatigue Syndrome
overview: A long-term illness marked by extreme fatigue that isn't relieved by rest.

name: Bronchial Asthma
overview: A chronic respiratory condition marked by airway inflammation, wheezing, and breathing difficulty.

name: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD)
overview: A group of lung diseases that block airflow and make breathing progressively difficult.

name: Obstructive Sleep Apnea
overview: A type of sleep apnea in which the airway repeatedly becomes blocked during sleep.

name: Crohn’s Disease
overview: A chronic inflammatory bowel disease that can affect any part of the digestive tract.

name: Ulcerative Colitis
overview: A chronic inflammation of the colon lining that causes ulcers and pain.

name: Dementia
overview: A group of symptoms affecting memory, thinking, and social abilities significantly enough to interfere with daily life.

name: Concussion
overview: A mild traumatic brain injury caused by a blow to the head, resulting in temporary brain dysfunction.

name: Otitis Media
overview: An infection or inflammation of the middle ear that often affects children.

name: Dental Abscess
overview: A painful infection around the root of a tooth or in the gums.

name: Viral Hepatitis
overview: Inflammation of the liver caused by several different viruses, including A, B, and C.

name: Cholecystitis
overview: Inflammation of the gallbladder causing upper abdominal pain and nausea.

name: Allergic Rhinitis
overview: An allergic reaction to airborne substances that causes nasal congestion and sneezing.

name: Atopic Dermatitis
overview: A chronic form of eczema causing itchy, inflamed skin that can flare periodically.

name: Plantar Fasciitis
overview: Inflammation of the tissue under the foot causing heel pain, especially with the first steps in the morning.

name: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome
overview: A condition caused by pressure on the median nerve in the wrist, leading to numbness and tingling in the hand.

name: Tendonitis
overview: Inflammation of a tendon that causes pain and tenderness near a joint.

name: Sciatica
overview: Pain that travels along the sciatic nerve from the lower back down the leg.

name: Carotid Artery Disease
overview: Narrowing of the carotid arteries that can reduce blood flow to the brain and raise stroke risk.

name: Peripheral Artery Disease
overview: A circulatory condition in which narrowed arteries reduce blood flow to the limbs.

name: Angina
overview: Chest pain or discomfort that occurs when the heart muscle does not get enough oxygen-rich blood.

name: Heart Attack
overview: A medical emergency in which blood flow to part of the heart is blocked, damaging the heart muscle.

name: High Cholesterol
overview: Elevated cholesterol levels in the blood that increase the risk of heart disease.

name: Varicose Veins
overview: Swollen, twisted veins that are visible under the skin, often in the legs.

name: Hives
overview: Raised itchy welts that appear on the skin as an allergic reaction.

name: Food Allergy
overview: An immune system reaction to a specific food that can cause symptoms ranging from mild to life-threatening.

name: Anaphylaxis
overview: A severe, potentially life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate emergency care.

name: Allergic Conjunctivitis
overview: Inflammation of the eye lining due to allergens, causing redness and itching.

name: Pulmonary Tuberculosis
overview: A lung infection caused by tuberculosis bacteria that can be contagious and persistent.
